              </mrow>
              <mrow class="MJX-TeXAtom-ORD">
                <mn>2</mn>
              </mrow>
            </msup>
            <mo>&#x2212;<!-- − --></mo>
            <msup>
              <mi>Q</mi>
              <mrow class="MJX-TeXAtom-ORD">
                <mn>2</mn>
              </mrow>
            </msup>
          </msqrt>
        </mrow>
        <mo>.</mo>
      </mstyle>
    </mrow>
    <annotation encoding="application/x-tex">{\displaystyle M+{\sqrt {M^{2}-{(J/M)}^{2}-Q^{2}}}.}</annotation>
  </semantics>
</math></span><img src="https://wikimedia.org/api/rest_v1/media/math/render/svg/3f88d017c9e4ee6f46de606ed152f56ed5f5c3a4" class="mwe-math-fallback-image-inline mw-invert skin-invert" aria-hidden="true" style="vertical-align: -1.671ex; width:28.319ex; height:4.843ex;" alt="{\displaystyle M+{\sqrt {M^{2}-{(J/M)}^{2}-Q^{2}}}.}"></span></span>
</li>
<li id="cite&#95;note-163"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-163">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text">The set of possible paths, or more accurately the future <a href="/wiki/Light_cone" title="Light cone">light cone</a> containing all possible <a href="/wiki/World_line" title="World line">world lines</a> (in this diagram the light cone is represented by the V-shaped region bounded by arrows representing light ray world lines), is tilted in this way in <a href="/wiki/Eddington%E2%80%93Finkelstein_coordinates" title="Eddington–Finkelstein coordinates">Eddington–Finkelstein coordinates</a> (the diagram is a "cartoon" version of an Eddington–Finkelstein coordinate diagram), but in other coordinates the light cones are not tilted in this way, for example in <a href="/wiki/Schwarzschild_coordinates" title="Schwarzschild coordinates">Schwarzschild coordinates</a> they narrow without tilting as one approaches the event horizon, and in <a href="/wiki/Kruskal%E2%80%93Szekeres_coordinates" title="Kruskal–Szekeres coordinates">Kruskal–Szekeres coordinates</a> the light cones do not change shape or orientation at all.<sup id="cite&#95;ref-Misner-1973&#95;136-1" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-Misner-1973-136"><span class="cite-bracket">&#91;</span>136<span class="cite-bracket">&#93;</span></a></sup><sup class="reference nowrap"><span title="Page: 848">&#58;&#8202;848&#8202;</span></sup></span>
</li>
</ol></div></div>

<li id="cite&#95;note-Throne-1994-7"><span class="mw-cite-backlink">^ <a href="#cite_ref-Throne-1994_7-0"><sup><i><b>a</b></i></sup></a> <a href="#cite_ref-Throne-1994_7-1"><sup><i><b>b</b></i></sup></a> <a href="#cite_ref-Throne-1994_7-2"><sup><i><b>c</b></i></sup></a> <a href="#cite_ref-Throne-1994_7-3"><sup><i><b>d</b></i></sup></a> <a href="#cite_ref-Throne-1994_7-4"><sup><i><b>e</b></i></sup></a> <a href="#cite_ref-Throne-1994_7-5"><sup><i><b>f</b></i></sup></a> <a href="#cite_ref-Throne-1994_7-6"><sup><i><b>g</b></i></sup></a> <a href="#cite_ref-Throne-1994_7-7"><sup><i><b>h</b></i></sup></a> <a href="#cite_ref-Throne-1994_7-8"><sup><i><b>i</b></i></sup></a> <a href="#cite_ref-Throne-1994_7-9"><sup><i><b>j</b></i></sup></a> <a href="#cite_ref-Throne-1994_7-10"><sup><i><b>k</b></i></sup></a> <a href="#cite_ref-Throne-1994_7-11"><sup><i><b>l</b></i></sup></a></span> <span class="reference-text"><link rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style" href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r1333433106" /><cite id="CITEREFThorneHawking1994" class="citation book cs1"><a href="/wiki/Kip_Thorne" title="Kip Thorne">Thorne, Kip S.</a>; <a href="/wiki/Stephen_Hawking" title="Stephen Hawking">Hawking, Stephen</a> (1994). "Cosmic Censorship and the Evolution of <span class="mwe-math-element mwe-math-element-inline"><span class="mwe-math-mathml-inline mwe-math-mathml-a11y" style="display: none;"><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"  alttext="{\displaystyle d}">

<li id="cite&#95;note-227"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-227">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style" href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r1333433106" /><cite id="CITEREFCattaneoFaberBinneyDekel2009" class="citation journal cs1">Cattaneo, A.; Faber, S. <li id="cite&#95;note-228"><span class="mw-cite-backlink"><b><a href="#cite_ref-228">^</a></b></span> <span class="reference-text"><link rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style" href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r1333433106" /><cite id="CITEREFRuizMolinaViloria2019" class="citation journal cs1">Ruiz, O.; Molina, U.; Viloria, P. (2019). "Thermodynamic Analysis of Kerr-Newman Black Holes". <i>Journal of Physics: Conference Series</i>. <b>1219</b> (1) 012016. <a href="/wiki/Bibcode_(identifier)" class="mw-redirect" title="Bibcode (identifier)">Bibcode</a>:<a rel="nofollow" class="external text" href="https://ui.adsabs.harvard.edu/abs/2019JPhCS1219a2016R">2019JPhCS1219a2016R</a>. <a href="/wiki/Doi_(identifier)" class="mw-redirect" title="Doi (identifier)">doi</a>:<a rel="nofollow" class="external text" href="https://doi.org/10.1088%2F1742-6596%2F1219%2F1%2F012016">10.1088/1742-6596/1219/1/012016</a>. <q>From this, an expression is established for the Hawking temperature of a Kerr-Newman black hole as a function of its mass 𝑀, angular moment 𝐽 and load 𝑄. As the black hole loses mass, its temperature increases inversely proportional.</q></cite><span title="ctx&#95;ver=Z39.88-2004&amp;rft&#95;val&#95;fmt=info%3Aofi%2Ffmt%3Akev%3Amtx%3Ajournal&amp;rft.genre=article&amp;rft.jtitle=Journal+of+Physics%3A+Conference+Series&amp;rft.atitle=Thermodynamic+Analysis+of+Kerr-Newman+Black+Holes&amp;rft.volume=1219&amp;rft.issue=1&amp;rft.artnum=012016&amp;rft.date=2019&amp;rft&#95;id=info%3Adoi%2F10.1088%2F1742-6596%2F1219%2F1%2F012016&amp;rft&#95;id=info%3Abibcode%2F2019JPhCS1219a2016R&amp;rft.aulast=Ruiz&amp;rft.aufirst=O.&amp;rft.au=Molina%2C+U.&amp;rft.au=Viloria%2C+P.&amp;rfr&#95;id=info%3Asid%2Fen.wikipedia.org%3ABlack+hole" class="Z3988"></span></span>

<div class="side-box-image"><span class="noviewer" typeof="mw:File"><a href="/wiki/File:Scholia_logo.svg" class="mw-file-description"><img alt="logo" src="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/32/Scholia_logo.svg/40px-Scholia_logo.svg.png" decoding="async" width="40" height="39" class="mw-file-element" srcset="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/32/Scholia_logo.svg/120px-Scholia_logo.svg.png 2x" data-file-width="107" data-file-height="104" /></a></span></div>
<div class="side-box-text plainlist"><a href="https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Wikidata:Scholia" class="extiw" title="d:Wikidata:Scholia">Scholia</a> has a profile for <a href="https://iw.toolforge.org/scholia/Q589" class="extiw" title="toolforge:scholia/Q589"><b>black hole <small>(Q589)</small></b></a>.</div></div>

<li><a href="/wiki/ESA" class="mw-redirect" title="ESA">ESA</a>'s <a rel="nofollow" class="external text" href="https://www.esa.int/gsp/ACT/phy/Projects/Blackholes/WebGL/">Black Hole Visualization</a> <a rel="nofollow" class="external text" href="https://web.archive.org/web/20190503070935/https://www.esa.int/gsp/ACT/phy/Projects/Blackholes/WebGL.html">Archived</a> 3 May 2019 at the <a href="/wiki/Wayback_Machine" title="Wayback Machine">Wayback Machine</a></li>
<li><a rel="nofollow" class="external text" href="https://web.archive.org/web/19980118023013/http://casa.colorado.edu/~ajsh/schw.shtml">Fall Into A Black Hole</a> on Andrew Hamilton's website</li>

<ul><li><a rel="nofollow" class="external text" href="https://www.pbs.org/video/black-hole-apocalypse-yj34qi/"><i>Black Hole Apocalypse</i></a> – documentary on <a href="/wiki/Nova_(American_TV_program)" title="Nova (American TV program)">NOVA</a></li>
<li><a rel="nofollow" class="external text" href="https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLsPUh22kYmNBl4h0i4mI5zDflExXJMo_x">Black Holes Playlist</a> on YouTube from <i><a href="/wiki/PBS_Space_Time" class="mw-redirect" title="PBS Space Time">PBS Space Time</a></i></li>
<li><a rel="nofollow" class="external text" href="https://www.bbc.com/news/science-environment-35524440">Computer Visualisation of a Signal Detected by LIGO</a> – artistic visualization of gravitational waves from merging black holes</li>
<li><a rel="nofollow" class="external text" href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=I_88S8DWbcU">Two Black Holes Merge Into One (Based Upon the Signal GW150914)</a> – realistic simulation of merging black holes</li>
<li><a rel="nofollow" class="external text" href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=crXGmeWFb9o">Plunge Into A Black Hole</a> – 360° NASA simulation and <a rel="nofollow" class="external text" href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=chhcwk4-esM">explanation</a></li></ul>
